meaningExtended: A verb meaning to shine brightly or excessively, to outshine, or to be excessively pleasing or attractive. It is formed from the prefix 'ati' (excessive, beyond) combined with the root 'ruc' (to shine, to please).

sentences.english: The sun shines excessively among all the stars.
His virtues shone excessively in the past.
The splendor of the sacrifice will shine excessively.

sentences.devanagari: कিं न तव बुद्धिः सर्वेषु अतिरोचते?
sentences.transliteration: kiṁ na tava buddhiḥ sarveṣu atirocate?
sentences.english: Does not your intellect shine excessively among all?
For what reason does the Lord's form shine so excessively?

sentences.devanagari: भो दोस्त! तव कीर्तिः तु सर्वत्र अतिरोचते।
sentences.transliteration: bho dost! tava kīrtiḥ tu sarvatra atirocate.
sentences.english: O friend! Your fame indeed shines excessively everywhere.

sentences.devanagari: आचार्य! तेन ज्ञानं न अतिरोचते इति।
sentences.transliteration: ācārya! tena jñānaṁ na atirocate iti.
sentences.english: Teacher! Knowledge does not shine excessively through him, so it is said.
Both of them outshine the poets among all.
All of them shine excessively like stars.

sentences.devanagari: यत्र सत्यं अतिरोचते, तत्र ब्रह्म विद्यते।
sentences.transliteration: yatra satyaṁ atirocate, tatra brahma vidyate.
sentences.english: Where truth shines excessively, there Brahman is found.
